title: filago carpetana subsp. carpetana
intro: Filago carpetana subsp. carpetana, also known as the false flax or fleawort, is a plant species belonging to the Asteraceae family. It is a perennial herb with small, yellow flowers and has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, such as digestive problems, wounds, and skin conditions, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and promote healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or taken internally to reap its health benefits.
